'''Nick Fozzard''' (born 22 July 1977) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for the [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Warrington Wolves]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Castleford Tigers]], and in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for the [[Dewsbury Rams]].

==Career==
===Early career===
Born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, Fozzard signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 1993 from amateur club Shaw Cross,<ref>{{cite web |last1=Richards |first1=Martin |title=Wolves fight to keep Fozzard from predators |url=https://www.thetimes.com/sport/football/article/wolves-fight-to-keep-fozzard-from-predators-vbw6z2rnd6b |website=[[The Times]] |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=19 July 2003}}</ref> and made his first team debut in April 1994 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web |title=Former Players receive Heritage Number Certificates |url=https://www.leedsrhinosfoundation.org/former-players-receive-heritage-number-certificates/ |website=Leeds Rhinos Foundation |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=22 March 2016}}</ref> He was signed by [[Huddersfield Giants]] in May 1997.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Wigan make pledge to shareholders |url=https://archive.org/details/Independent1997IrelandEnglish/May%2016%201997%2C%20Independent%2C%20%233298%2C%20Ireland%20%28en%29/page/n27/mode/2up?view=theater |work=The Independent |date=16 May 1997 |page=29}}</ref> Fozzard missed almost two full seasons after suffering a broken arm in a match against [[London Broncos]] in March 2000,<ref>{{cite web |title=Broncos tough it out |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_league/683069.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=19 March 2000}}</ref> and then breaking his arm a second time 10 months later.<ref>{{cite news |title=Sport in brief |work=The Guardian |date=3 January 2001 |location=London |page=26 |id={{ProQuest|188553532}}}}</ref> He joined [[Warrington Wolves]] in 2002,<ref>{{cite web |title=Propping up Wolves |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5281496.propping-up-wolves/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=13 September 2001}}</ref> before signing for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fozzard signs for Saints |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3096009.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=25 July 2003}}</ref>

===St Helens===
[[File:Zeb Luisi & Nick Fozzard.JPG|thumb|left|100px|Fozzard about to tackle [[Zeb Luisi]] against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] at the [[Twickenham Stoop]]]]
Fozzard played for Saints in their 32–16 win against [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[2004 Challenge Cup]] final.<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints win Challenge Cup |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/3711731.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=15 May 2004}}</ref>

In 2005, Fozzard was one of five uncapped players selected by [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] for the [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2005 Tri-Nations]].<ref>{{cite web |title=No fear of Kiwi pack says Fozzard |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4375768.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=25 October 2005}}</ref> He received his one and only cap for the team in the opening match of the tournament, a 26–42 defeat against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Great Britain 26–42 New Zealand |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4384750.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=29 October 2005}}</ref>

In 2006, Fozzard extended his contract with Saints until the end of 2008.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fozzard signs new St Helens deal |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/5059306.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=8 June 2006}}</ref> Due to increased competition for places, he missed out on St Helens [[2006 Challenge Cup]] final victory.<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints to leave out Anderson and Fozzard |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/saints-to-leave-out-anderson-and-fozzard-413073.html |website=The Independent |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=24 August 2006}}</ref>

As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Fozzard played as a {{rlp|pr}} in St. Helens' 18–14 victory. He also won his second Challenge Cup with the club, starting in the 30–8 win against [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[2007 Challenge Cup]] final,<ref>{{cite web |title=Showpiece sacrificed to success as Saints gear up for the double |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/aug/27/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=27 August 2007}}</ref> and was selected in the 2007 [[Super League Dream Team]] at the end of the season.

Fozzard missed most of the 2008 season after suffering a serious knee injury.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fozzard in need of knee surgery |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/7263135.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=25 February 2008}}</ref> He returned in time for the end of the season, helping the team secure a fourth consecutive [[League Leaders' Shield]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints seal leaders' shield but defiant Wigan take their share |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/sep/06/superleague.sthelens |website=The Guardian |access-date=15 February 2024 |date=6 September 2008}}</ref> He also played in the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008}}</ref>

===Later career===
On 19 September 2008 Fozzard signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the 2009 Super League campaign.

It was confirmed on 20 September 2009, that Fozzard would return to St. Helens after being released by Hull Kingston Rovers on the final year of his contract.<ref name="Fozzard returns to Saints">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15387|title=Fozzard returns to Saints|publisher=superleague.com|date=20 September 2009|access-date=20 September 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090923200751/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15387|archive-date=23 September 2009}}</ref> Following a poor start to the 2010 season, Fozzard was dropped by coach Mick Potter.

Fozzard joined [[Castleford Tigers]] on a one-year deal on 16 September 2010. He retired in 2012 due to a serious shoulder injury.<ref name="Ex-Great Britain forward Nick Fozzard retires from rugby league">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17352874|title=Ex-Great Britain forward Nick Fozzard retires from rugby league|publisher=BBC|date=13 March 2012|access-date=1 January 2013}}</ref>
